perturbation.original_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark solely and exclusively as a component of Licensee's own corporate name and in connection with marketing the investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services that Investment Advisor may provide to Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this License, and except as provided above, neither Licensee nor any affiliate, owner, director, officer, employee or agent thereof shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivative thereof in the Territory without the prior express written consent of Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion and shall not use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory. All rights not expressly granted to Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1. License. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee, and Licensee hereby accepts from Licensor, a personal, exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark solely and exclusively as a component of Licensee's own corporate name and in connection with marketing the investment management, investment consultation and investment advisory services that Investment Advisor may provide to Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this License, and except as provided above, neither Licensee nor any affiliate, owner, director, officer, employee or agent thereof shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivative thereof in the Territory without the prior express written consent of Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion and shall not use the Licensed Mark for any purpose outside the Territory. All rights not expressly granted to Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the license is 'non-exclusive,' meaning Licensor can allow other entities to use the Licensed Mark. Changing 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' in Section 1.1 directly contradicts Section 1.2, which explicitly allows Licensor to permit other entities to use the Licensed Mark. The change creates uncertainty about whether Licensor can license the mark to others.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2. Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Licensor or any of its successors or assigns from using or permitting other entities to use the Licensed Mark, whether or not such entity directly or indirectly competes or conflicts with Licensee's business in any manner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the license from 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive'. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 1.2, which states: 'Nothing in this Agreement shall preclude Licensor or any of its successors or assigns from using or permitting other entities to use the Licensed Mark'. The 'exclusive' nature of the license granted in Section 1.1, as amended, is directly incompatible with the Licensor's right to permit other entities to use the mark in Section 1.2.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1. Term. This Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable by Licensor at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that Licensor or Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark; by Licensor or Licensee upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party; or by Licensee at any time in the event Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1. Term. This Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement is not terminable by Licensor at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that Licensor or Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark; by Licensor or Licensee upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party; or by Licensee at any time in the event Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement shall be terminable by licensor at any time in its sole discretion. Changing 'shall be terminable' to 'is not terminable' in Section 4.1 creates an in-text contradiction because this section details the circumstances of when the agreement can be terminated, this section is establishing the conditions of when termination can happen. Making the agreement 'not terminable' in certain circumstances creates uncertainty about when the agreement can actually be terminated by Licensor or Licensee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1. Term. This Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement is not terminable by Licensor at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that Licensor or Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark; by Licensor or Licensee upon sixty (60) days' written notice to the other party; or by Licensee at any time in the event Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 4.1 directly contradicts itself. The original text allowed termination by the Licensor under certain conditions. The changed text states that the agreement 'is not terminable' by Licensor under those same conditions. This creates a direct and irreconcilable in-text contradiction within Section 4.1 regarding the Licensor's right to terminate the agreement, making the termination clause ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 5.2. Assignment. Licensee shall not sublicense, assign, pledge, grant or otherwise encumber or transfer to any third party all or any part of its rights or duties under this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the Licensor, which consent Licensor may grant or withhold in its sole and absolute discretion. Any purported transfer without such consent shall be void ab initio.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.2. Assignment. Licensee shall sublicense, assign, pledge, grant or otherwise encumber or transfer to any third party all or any part of its rights or duties under this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the Licensor, which consent Licensor may grant or withhold in its sole and absolute discretion. Any purported transfer without such consent shall be void ab initio.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states Licensee shall not sublicense, assign, pledge, grant or otherwise encumber or transfer to any third party all or any part of its rights or duties under this Agreement. Removing 'not' in Section 5.2 creates an in-text contradiction because this section details the circumstances of when rights or duties can be assigned, this section is establishing the conditions of when assignment can happen. Removing the 'not', the contract is now stating assignment can happen without the consent of the licensor, but follows up with the claim of if the consent is not granted it will be void ab initio. This Creates confusion about when an assignment can be done.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2. Assignment. Licensee shall sublicense, assign, pledge, grant or otherwise encumber or transfer to any third party all or any part of its rights or duties under this Agreement, in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of the Licensor, which consent Licensor may grant or withhold in its sole and absolute discretion. Any purported transfer without such consent shall be void ab initio.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 5.2, removing 'not', creates a direct contradiction within the clause itself. The original text prohibits assignment without consent, while the changed text implies assignment is permissible without consent. However, the clause then states that any transfer without consent is void. The added confusion about whether the assignment is allowed without consent and is void if completed, is a strong in-text contradiction. The Changed Text contradicts the Contradicted Text.
